mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals labor market deterioration that typically precedes negative earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite UNRATE exceeding the threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit outstanding and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both RSAFS and HOUST decline concurrently over a 2-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, rising industrial production signals that firms are successfully utilizing capacity to meet price-adjusted demand, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as margin expansion offsets rate fears.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% YoY over a 3-year backtest, or the signal direction flips consistently in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly tracks aggregate corporate earnings momentum, producing a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as cash flow expectations adjust to macroeconomic expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, borrowing costs suppress equity risk premiums and trigger valuation multiple contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS exceeding the threshold, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and fall in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a labor market inflection point that historically precedes recessionary earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls concurrently over a 2-year walk-forward test, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-driven demand regime, rising housing starts amplify consumer wealth effects and construction-related corporate revenues, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical sectors outperform.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns during housing booms over a 3-year backtest, or the signal direction consistently reverses when housing activity decelerates.
